I eat 8 pounds of grass a day. I'm built different. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. The right match is someone who values presence over performance. I value this deeply. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Nico
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. I was born in the wet season. It affected my personality in ways I am still discovering. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. I'm not going anywhere. That's a feature, not a flaw.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Bonita
bio:
Sun-dried fur, good vibes, open to adventure 🌞. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. I believe in long mornings, shared snacks, and not explaining yourself unless asked. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Javier
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. My mornings start slow and I prefer it that way. The grass is better when you take your time with it. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I take things slow on purpose. The destination is better that way.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Gordita
bio:
Fluffy, grassy, a little damp. Perfect honestly. I came from a large family. We were all in the same river. I got out first. Ideally you're the kind of person who doesn't need to fill every silence. Silences are good here. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Pelusa
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. Find me by the third reed. I'll be there.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Ronaldo
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I hope you're well. I hope the water near you is warm.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Aquiles
bio:
I let birds sit on me. Very chill energy only please. People describe me as grounding. I describe myself as wet, occasionally, and fine with it. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Pancake
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Isabella
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. I have strong feelings about the water temperature and I am not afraid to express them. Ideally you're the kind of person who doesn't need to fill every silence. Silences are good here. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Donut
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. I spend most of my days at the edge of the water, watching things unfold without getting involved. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. Fair warning: I will remember everything you say near the water.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Tank
bio:
Looking for someone to float down the river with at sunset 🌅. I spend most of my days at the edge of the water, watching things unfold without getting involved. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I hope this was enough to know. If not, ask. I answer eventually.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Pebble
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I am not hard to find. I am usually in the same spot by the third reed from the left. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I will share my grass. That is a significant offer. Please take it seriously.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Gabriela
bio:
Raised by the Amazon. Unbothered. Moisturized. Thriving. My friends say I'm the calm one. My acquaintances say I'm the quiet one. Both groups are right. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. I will share my grass. That is a significant offer. Please take it seriously.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. I don't need much. Good water, soft grass, someone who doesn't rush me. That's the whole list. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I'm not going anywhere. That's a feature, not a flaw.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Capito
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. I'm a morning capy. By afternoon I am horizontal. By evening I am philosophical. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Sofia
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. My friends say I'm the calm one. My acquaintances say I'm the quiet one. Both groups are right. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Peludo
bio:
Looking for someone to float down the river with at sunset 🌅. The reeds near my usual spot are particularly good this season. I noticed. I thought you should know. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. Come say hello. The reeds are good today.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Macho
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I came from a large family. We were all in the same river. I got out first. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Senor
bio:
I eat 8 pounds of grass a day. I'm built different. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. I don't want much from a relationship. Just presence, warmth, and at least one shared mud bath. You'll know it's working when the silence feels comfortable.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Thicc Boi
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. Looking for someone who understands that stillness is not the same as boredom. My hobbies include the ones listed above and also just being near things.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Ronaldo
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I hope you're well. I hope the water near you is warm.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. I am selective about where I sit. This tells you everything about me. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. My hobbies include the ones listed above and also just being near things.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Aquiles
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Pilar
bio:
I'm the therapist friend but make it capybara. I have been told I radiate calm. I have also been told I radiate warmth. Both are accurate and I'm proud. A good match for me is someone who has strong feelings about at least one small, specific thing. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Sir Fluff
bio:
I let birds sit on me. Very chill energy only please. My friends say I'm the calm one. My acquaintances say I'm the quiet one. Both groups are right. What I'm looking for is someone who matches my energy — which is to say, low and consistent. I take things slow on purpose. The destination is better that way.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Honey
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. The right match is someone who values presence over performance. I value this deeply. The territory comes with me. It's a package deal.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Bonita
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. I prefer rivers to ponds. Ponds are fine. Rivers have more going on. Tell me something real. Not something impressive. Something real. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

Will share my mud bath with the right one. I prefer rivers to ponds. Ponds are fine. Rivers have more going on. Looking for someone who understands that stillness is not the same as boredom. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Peludo
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. I will share my grass. That is a significant offer. Please take it seriously.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Chula
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Gordita
bio:
Looking for a herbivore with ambition 🥬. I was born in the wet season. It affected my personality in ways I am still discovering. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Big Chungus Jr
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. My mornings start slow and I prefer it that way. The grass is better when you take your time with it. Tell me something real. Not something impressive. Something real. My hobbies include the ones listed above and also just being near things.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Thicc Boi
bio:
I eat 8 pounds of grass a day. I'm built different. I have been told I radiate calm. I have also been told I radiate warmth. Both are accurate and I'm proud. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. My hobbies include the ones listed above and also just being near things.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Capuchina
bio:
I'm told I have a calming aura. By everyone. Including the crocodiles 🐊. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. The territory comes with me. It's a package deal.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Aquiles
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. People describe me as grounding. I describe myself as wet, occasionally, and fine with it. I am good at listening. I am better at sitting quietly nearby while you figure things out. I take things slow on purpose. The destination is better that way.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I am selective about where I sit. This tells you everything about me. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. Fair warning: I will remember everything you say near the water.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Rico
bio:
I let birds sit on me. Very chill energy only please. I'm a morning capy. By afternoon I am horizontal. By evening I am philosophical. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Hector
bio:
Will share my mud bath with the right one. I have been told I radiate calm. I have also been told I radiate warmth. Both are accurate and I'm proud. I want someone who will sit with me until the light changes. That's the whole pitch. I will share my grass. That is a significant offer. Please take it seriously.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

name: Fernanda
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. A good match for me is someone who has strong feelings about at least one small, specific thing. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Pilar
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

bio:

name: Javier
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. The territory comes with me. It's a package deal.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

bio:

name: Chula
bio:
I'm told I have a calming aura. By everyone. Including the crocodiles 🐊. I came from a large family. We were all in the same river. I got out first. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I'm not going anywhere. That's a feature, not a flaw.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Dante
bio:

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. I don't need much. Good water, soft grass, someone who doesn't rush me. That's the whole list. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. I hope you're well. I hope the water near you is warm.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Bonita
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. A good match for me is someone who has strong feelings about at least one small, specific thing. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Nacho
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I have never regretted sitting somewhere beautiful. Come sit somewhere beautiful with me.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Big Chungus Jr
bio:
Full-figured, full of grass, full of love 💕. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. I'm not looking for adventure. I'm looking for someone to sit with while adventure happens nearby. Come say hello. The reeds are good today.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Javier
bio:
Looking for someone to float down the river with at sunset 🌅. The thing about me is that I never rush. I have never once rushed. The world is better for it. I don't want much from a relationship. Just presence, warmth, and at least one shared mud bath. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Carlos
bio:
Looking for someone to float down the river with at sunset 🌅. I am selective about where I sit. This tells you everything about me. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

bio:

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

Will share my mud bath with the right one. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Chula
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. I prefer rivers to ponds. Ponds are fine. Rivers have more going on. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Hector
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. The right match is someone who values presence over performance. I value this deeply. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Marco
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I'm not going anywhere. That's a feature, not a flaw.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Fernanda
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. My best quality is that I never panic. My worst quality is that this sometimes reads as indifference. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. Find me by the third reed. I'll be there.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Fluffita
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. The territory comes with me. It's a package deal.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

Looking for a herbivore with ambition 🥬. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Donatella
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I have strong feelings about the water temperature and I am not afraid to express them. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I hope this was enough to know. If not, ask. I answer eventually.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Nicolas
bio:
I let birds sit on me. Very chill energy only please. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. I don't want much from a relationship. Just presence, warmth, and at least one shared mud bath. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Diana
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. My friends say I'm the calm one. My acquaintances say I'm the quiet one. Both groups are right. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. Come find me. I'll be by the water. I'm always by the water.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Bernardo
bio:
I'm the therapist friend but make it capybara. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Pablo
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. My best quality is that I never panic. My worst quality is that this sometimes reads as indifference. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. Come say hello. The reeds are good today.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Rosa
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. My best quality is that I never panic. My worst quality is that this sometimes reads as indifference. What I'm looking for is someone who matches my energy — which is to say, low and consistent. Come say hello. The reeds are good today.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Rico
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. I am not hard to find. I am usually in the same spot by the third reed from the left. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. I have never regretted sitting somewhere beautiful. Come sit somewhere beautiful with me.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Waffles
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. My mornings start slow and I prefer it that way. The grass is better when you take your time with it. Tell me something real. Not something impressive. Something real. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

I do not need you to be impressive. I need you to be present. Those are different things and most creatures confuse them. Presence is harder to manufacture and worth considerably more.

name: Sir Fluff
bio:
Looking for someone to float down the river with at sunset 🌅. I'm a morning capy. By afternoon I am horizontal. By evening I am philosophical. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Estrella
bio:
Sun-dried fur, good vibes, open to adventure 🌞. I spend most of my days at the edge of the water, watching things unfold without getting involved. I am good at listening. I am better at sitting quietly nearby while you figure things out. I hope this was enough to know. If not, ask. I answer eventually.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Big Chungus Jr
bio:
I let birds sit on me. Very chill energy only please. I don't need much. Good water, soft grass, someone who doesn't rush me. That's the whole list. A good match for me is someone who has strong feelings about at least one small, specific thing. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Coco
bio:

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

I'm the therapist friend but make it capybara. People describe me as grounding. I describe myself as wet, occasionally, and fine with it. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. I have never regretted sitting somewhere beautiful. Come sit somewhere beautiful with me.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Big Chungus Jr
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Big Chungus Jr
bio:
I'm told I have a calming aura. By everyone. Including the crocodiles 🐊. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. Looking for someone who understands that stillness is not the same as boredom. I have never regretted sitting somewhere beautiful. Come sit somewhere beautiful with me.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

name: Gordo
bio:

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

name: Valentina
bio:

A good match for me is someone who has strong feelings about at least one small, specific thing. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

I eat 8 pounds of grass a day. I'm built different. There are few problems that a good mud patch cannot solve. I have tested this theory extensively. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. Come find me. I'll be by the water. I'm always by the water.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Carmen
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. I have never regretted sitting somewhere beautiful. Come sit somewhere beautiful with me.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Nacho
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I am not hard to find. I am usually in the same spot by the third reed from the left. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I hope you're well. I hope the water near you is warm.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Cappuccina
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. I was born in the wet season. It affected my personality in ways I am still discovering. I believe in long mornings, shared snacks, and not explaining yourself unless asked. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

name: Gordita
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I don't chase. I wait. The right ones always come back to the river.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Fernanda
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I was born in the wet season. It affected my personality in ways I am still discovering. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. Come find me. I'll be by the water. I'm always by the water.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Nacho
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. There are few problems that a good mud patch cannot solve. I have tested this theory extensively. Tell me something real. Not something impressive. Something real. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Pancake
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. There are few problems that a good mud patch cannot solve. I have tested this theory extensively. I'd like to share my territory with someone who respects a slow pace and appreciates good grass. My standards are simple and non-negotiable. Yours probably are too. Let's compare.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Bruno
bio:

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I'm a morning capy. By afternoon I am horizontal. By evening I am philosophical. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. You'll know it's working when the silence feels comfortable.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Pumpkin
bio:
I eat 8 pounds of grass a day. I'm built different. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. I believe in long mornings, shared snacks, and not explaining yourself unless asked. I will share my grass. That is a significant offer. Please take it seriously.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

name: Churrita
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. There are few problems that a good mud patch cannot solve. I have tested this theory extensively. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Zuzu
bio:
I'm the therapist friend but make it capybara. The thing about me is that I never rush. I have never once rushed. The world is better for it. Ideally you're the kind of person who doesn't need to fill every silence. Silences are good here. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Ernesto
bio:
River > everything. Come float with me. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. If you can coexist peacefully with the birds that land on my back, we'll get along fine. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Josefina
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I came from a large family. We were all in the same river. I got out first. I want someone who finds the river beautiful at dawn. I am there at dawn. Every single morning. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Pebble
bio:
I let small animals sit on my head. It's called abundance mindset. I don't need much. Good water, soft grass, someone who doesn't rush me. That's the whole list. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. I'm not going anywhere. That's a feature, not a flaw.

I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

I want a match who understands that the best parts of any day are not the events — they're the spaces between the events. The sitting. The watching. The quiet that isn't empty.

name: Fleurette
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. I believe in unhurried mornings and I believe in you if you're reading this. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Fleurette
bio:
Semi-aquatic and fully romantic. I am not hard to find. I am usually in the same spot by the third reed from the left. Not looking for perfection. Looking for someone who shows up and stays for a while. I've been told I'm an acquired taste. I think that's a compliment.

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

bio:

I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Churro
bio:

I am better at sitting quietly nearby while you figure things out. You'll know it's working when the silence feels comfortable.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I have strong opinions about where to sit and I will not apologize for them. The right spot makes everything better — conversation, thinking, rest. I have spent years finding the right spots and I am not moving.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

bio:

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

name: Pablo
bio:
Sun-dried fur, good vibes, open to adventure 🌞. I have opinions about mud quality that I will share whether you ask or not. I am good at listening. I am better at sitting quietly nearby while you figure things out. I will not message first. But I will be very glad when you do.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

I am not a complicated individual. My needs are specific, not many. Good water. Soft ground. Someone worth sharing the afternoon with. I have learned that simple needs, met consistently, produce something that looks a lot like contentment.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Tito
bio:
Big boy energy. Gentle giant. 10/10 would befriend a jaguar again. I have a specific place I like to sit and I will not explain why. You'll understand when you see it. What I offer: stability, warmth, a good spot by the river. What I want: the same back, approximately. I don't overthink things. This has served me well so far.

I once stayed in the same spot for four days during a particularly good stretch of weather. My companions thought something was wrong. Nothing was wrong. Sometimes the correct response to a good situation is to remain in it as long as possible.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Peludo
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. Very little disturbs me. This is not a boast. It's just how the water runs. Not looking for perfection. I've watched three generations of birds nest in the same tree above my resting spot. I don't know if they know I'm there. I know they're there. That kind of quiet coexistence is what I'm looking for in most things.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Pebble
bio:

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

Tell me something true about yourself. Not impressive. Not a best version. Something real. I will do the same. That seems like the right way to start.

name: Javier
bio:

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

My ideal arrangement involves long mornings, minimal urgency, shared territory that feels like a choice rather than a compromise, and at least one reliable daily moment where neither of us has anywhere to be.

I do not talk about myself easily. This is an exception. I made it because something about this felt worth the effort, and I have learned to trust that instinct when it shows up.

The thing I am most sure of: I would rather have one right match than many approximate ones. I have been waiting for the right one. I am still waiting. I think this message might be the beginning of something.

name: Cesar
bio:
I've been in 3 viral videos. Don't ask which ones. 🎬. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I'm patient. Genuinely. I have waited out three rainy seasons and I will wait for the right one too. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

The water near where I was born runs slow in the mornings and fast by afternoon. I think about that often. It shaped how I move through things — unhurried at the start, decisive when it counts.

If you want to know what I value: consistency. Not excitement. Not novelty. Consistency — the kind that makes a place feel like home and a presence feel like rest. That is the whole ask.

name: Biscuit
bio:
My personality is 'large mammal in shallow water' and I own it. My friends say I'm the calm one. My acquaintances say I'm the quiet one. Both groups are right. My ideal match is someone who finds depth in simple things. The water is deep. So am I. I'm not hard to impress. But I am hard to forget.

My territory is not large but I know every inch of it. The dry patch near the eastern reeds. The soft mud by the second bend. The grass that grows back fastest after the rain. I have been paying attention for years.

What I want is not complicated to describe but seems to be genuinely rare. Someone who sits with me and doesn't feel the need to fill the silence. Someone who comes back. Someone for whom showing up is a given, not a gesture.

name: Churrita
bio:
Professional grass eater. Amateur swimmer. Full-time vibe. I have been told I radiate calm. I have also been told I radiate warmth. Both are accurate and I'm proud. I want someone who will sit with me until the light changes. That's the whole pitch. I hope this was enough to know. If not, ask. I answer eventually.

I grew up in a large group. Seventeen of us at the peak season. I learned early that the most important skill is knowing when to be present and when to let others have the space. I still practice this.

I am not interested in performing. I have watched too many creatures perform — the display, the posture, the call that's slightly too loud. I would rather be less impressive and more real. I assume you feel the same or you wouldn't have read this far.

name: Churrita
bio:
Living my best life by the river. Looking for my pond partner. I practice stillness daily. Not as a discipline. Just as a way of being. Tell me something real. Not something impressive. Something real. I hope this was enough to know. If not, ask. I answer eventually.

The rainy season changes everything here. The river swells, the grass flattens, the birds relocate. Most creatures treat it as a disruption. I treat it as a reset. I find that perspective has served me well.

I have been patient for a long time. Not in a suffering way — in a deliberate way. The right things are worth waiting for and I have always believed this. I'm still here. That should tell you something.

name: Valentina
bio:
My friends call me The Tank. My mom calls me beautiful 💪. I am easygoing in theory and extremely specific in practice. These two things coexist peacefully. I appreciate directness. Say what you mean. The wetland is full of creatures who say nothing at all. If you made it this far, you already know if we're compatible.

There is a particular quality of light in the late afternoon that lands on water differently than any other time of day. I noticed this when I was young and I have never stopped noticing it. I find people who notice small things like that to be the most interesting kind.

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.

name: Gomez
bio:

What I bring: a good spot by the water, an honest disposition, and the kind of calm that other creatures seem to find either reassuring or boring, depending on who they are. I hope you're the first kind.
